steps.title: Recognize the complete second sentence
steps.content:
The passage provides a second sentence that begins with “Used to record length and volume, respectively, the sheep and heqat have since fallen out of use.” This sentence is already grammatically complete, with a participial phrase opening.

The question asks where the first sentence should end to maintain proper structure.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary
steps.content:
A period after “measurement” (choice B) ends the first sentence cleanly, allowing the second sentence to stand as its own complete thought.

Choices C and D include “used to record length and volume, respectively” in the first sentence as well, creating redundant information across both sentences.

Choice A is grammatically incorrect. That matches choice B — B is correct.
